## Eligibility.
To be considered for a grant, an organization must:
- Be a 501(c)3 under IRS code
- Provide a recent Form 990
- Provide audited financial statements
- Do charitable work within the state of Arizona
- Not discriminate based upon race, religion, sexual orientation, gender identity or immigration status
- Have an annual budget of $1,000,000 or more
- Have operated for at least three years
- Align with the Foundation’s specific areas of focus
Please Note: We are not currently seeking new proposals in the area of Military & First Responders.

Home / Our grants Our grants. The Bob & Renee Parsons Foundation was formed to reach low-income and underserved populations, marginalized communities, and causes often overlooked or underfunded by mainstream philanthropy. The Foundation focuses on the areas of youth & education, health & home, military & first responders, and the American dream. The Foundation provides transformational gifts to nonprofit organizations that are successfully addressing these areas and can demonstrate and measure the impact of their work. See if you are eligible Our process. To apply for a grant we’ll ask you to: FIRST Take an eligibility quiz. Ten short questions will verify your eligibility. SECOND Submit your letter of interest, providing additional information about your organization and the specific projects or campaigns you wish to fund. THIRD We’ll now take some time to carefully review your submission and conduct our due diligence using publicly available information. If we believe your organization may be a fit, we’ll be in touch within 30 days to learn more about your request and the work you do. If you do not hear from us within that time frame, however, it means your request is unfortunately not the right fit at this time. FOURTH Apply for a grant. You will receive an email invitation from Foundation staff with a link to our grants portal where you will create an account to complete a formal grant application. During this process you will: Submit a formal proposal Provide audited financial statements and other documents Complete a questionnaire to assess your organization, leadership and staff, vision and strategy, and financial health and outlook Get started Past grants. ## Our grants.
The Bob & Renee Parsons Foundation was formed to reach low-income and underserved populations, marginalized communities, and causes often overlooked or underfunded by mainstream philanthropy. The Foundation focuses on the areas of youth & education, health & home, military & first responders, and the American dream. The Foundation provides transformational gifts to nonprofit organizations that are successfully addressing these areas and can demonstrate and measure the impact of their work.
## Our process.
To apply for a grant we’ll ask you to:
## FIRST
Take an eligibility quiz. Ten short questions will verify your eligibility.
## SECOND
Submit your letter of interest, providing additional information about your organization and the specific projects or campaigns you wish to fund.
## THIRD
We’ll now take some time to carefully review your submission and conduct our due diligence using publicly available information. If we believe your organization may be a fit, we’ll be in touch within 30 days to learn more about your request and the work you do. If you do not hear from us within that time frame, however, it means your request is unfortunately not the right fit at this time.
## FOURTH
Apply for a grant. You will receive an email invitation from Foundation staff with a link to our grants portal where you will create an account to complete a formal grant application. During this process you will:
- Submit a formal proposal
- Provide audited financial statements and other documents
- Complete a questionnaire to assess your organization, leadership and staff, vision and strategy, and financial health and outlook

HOME ABOUT Who We Are Meet The Team What We Do How We Work Past Recipients Photo Gallery WHO WE HELP Cancer Patients Homeless Veterans Disaster Victims Individuals With Substance Abuse Victims of Gangs and Gun Violence Christian Ministries Local Humane Societies and Animal Shelters Sex and Human Trafficking GRANT SEEKERS CONTACT US EVENTS Select Page Our Mission To provide financial support to non-profit organizations, human and health services, homeless shelters, military veterans, religious causes, humane societies, sex and human trafficking, and victims of crimes and calamities. Our vision To serve with compassion and faith, uplifting lives through support for those in need and advancing the work of organizations grounded in hope, healing, and human dignity. LB CHARITABLE FOUNDATION PURPOSE This corporation is a nonprofit public benefit corporation and is not organized for the private gain of any person. It is organized under the California Nonprofit Public Benefit Corporation Law for public and charitable purposes. The specific purposes of this corporation are to support organizations that benefit the homeless, animal shelters, cancer research, disabled veterans, drug rehabilitation clinics, and victims of gun violence, and to engage in any other lawful activities permitted under the California Nonprofit Public Benefit Corporation Law. This corporation has been formed for such public and charitable purposes only. WHO WE ARE LBCF, at its core, is a family-led foundation whose primary objectives are philanthropy and social well-being (e.g. educational, religious, or other activities serving the public interest or common good). We work together to lead the foundation and create access to opportunity for people and surrounding communities. WHAT WE DO As we set our eyes on making an impact that improves lives today in surrounding communities and organizations, we also want to ensure that it lasts to benefit future generations. That’s why we here at LBCF are committed to the issues we work on for the long term, and why we’re constantly evaluating our efforts to make sure our investments and solutions are effective and long lasting. HOW WE WORK LBCF identifies important “need” gaps in our areas of interest or communities. Develops and tests promising solutions through financial contribution to narrow those gaps and then broadly share new ideas with the community or leadership. who we help Cure for Cancer and Other Diseases through provided support of Advanced Medical Research When it comes to cancer and other diseases we want to be an organization that is not only ready to help but to support financially. We know how much diseases and unexpected diagnoses can be out of our control but we want to focus on things we can control. Through grants and donations we can help your organization get moving on research and a cure. who we help THE HOMELESS AND COMMUNITY SHELTERS We here at LBCF don’t want anyone without a roof over their head. We want to battle and do our part in overcoming the homeless epidemic across the country. With efforts starting locally and expanding globally we know we can partner up with the right leaders and help those who are in need. who we help COMMUNITIES SUFFERING FROM NATURAL DISASTERS We here at LBCF don’t want anyone without a roof over their head. We want to battle and do our part in overcoming the homeless epidemic across the country. With efforts starting locally and expanding globally we know we can partner up with the right leaders and help those who are in need. who we help AID AND RELIEF TO DISABLED VETERANS We never want to take our freedom for granted and especially not to forget the sacrifice that was and is made everyday to protect our freedom. We want to help to make the transition from soldier to civilian a little easier. Uplifting and aiding veterans of all walks of life and experience to make them feel appreciated and never forgotten. who we help COMMUNITIES SUFFERING FROM NATURAL DISASTERS We want to rebuild, restructure and help when disaster happens in our country and surrounding communities. We not only are here when it happens but to prepare and fund strategies to help prevent disasters. With the parameters in place we can do our best to be ahead and ready. who we help COMMUNITIES SUFFERING FROM NATURAL DISASTERS We want to rebuild, restructure and help when disaster happens in our country and surrounding communities. We not only are here when it happens but to prepare and fund strategies to help prevent disasters. With the parameters in place we can do our best to be ahead and ready. who we help INDIVIDUALS SUFFERING FROM SUBSTANCE ABUSE PROBLEMS There’s not a lot of life halting things like a family member or people stuck in an addiction. We want to either fund or provide the right routes of recovery to the best of our abilities. We care about their well-being and want them to get on their feet. Professional help is expensive and we here at LBCF don’t want the lack of financials to be the reason someone cant get the help they need. who we help VICTIMS OF GANG AND GUN VIOLENCE The youth and the direction they are headed in is so vital to our community. With gangs and gun violence on the constant rise, we want to help. We want to be there when an unexpected gun injury to a loved one happens. Being a bridge from gangs and violence to a healthier path is what our aim is here at the LBCF. who we help VICTIMS OF GANG AND GUN VIOLENCE The youth and the direction they are headed in is so vital to our community. With gangs and gun violence on the constant rise, we want to help. We want to be there when an unexpected gun injury to a loved one happens. Being a bridge from gangs and violence to a healthier path is what our aim is here at the LBCF. who we help CHRISTIAN MINISTRIES AND OUTREACH EFFORTS Supporting the spread of the gospel of Jesus Christ throughout our world and community is with utmost importance here at LBCF. We know how much impact it can have and we are here to support any efforts locally or globally. With the spread of the hope and love that Jesus Christ offers we know that we can have an everlasting impact on the world. Supporting and providing means to make disciples through the great commission is one of our goals here at LBCF. who we help local humane societies and animal shelters We know and understand how important our animals are, not only to our homes but how they affect the communities and global resources. We want to aid the effort to keep our world and animal population healthy and thriving. Also helping with the training of service animals as well as training for the individual to receive one. who we help local humane societies and animal shelters We know and understand how important our animals are, not only to our homes but how they affect the communities and global resources. We want to aid the effort to keep our world and animal population healthy and thriving. Also helping with the training of service animals as well as training for the individual to receive one. who we help VICTIMS OF SEX TRAFFICKING AND HUMAN TRAFFICKING We recognize the devastating impact human trafficking has on individuals, families, and communities. As a modern form of slavery, sex trafficking exploits vulnerable people through force, fraud, or coercion for commercial sex acts. We aim to support survivors, raise awareness, and assist in prevention efforts. By addressing the root causes—such as child abuse, intimate partner violence, and youth exploitation—we help restore safety, dignity, and hope to those affected, including women, men, and children across the U.S. and the world. we can help GRANT SEEKERS FORM Interested in grant funding? Please fill out the contact form and we will be in touch as soon as possible. Our Mission To provide financial support to non-profit organizations, human and health services, homeless shelters, military veterans, religious causes, humane societies, sex and human trafficking, and victims of crimes and calamities. Our vision To serve with compassion and faith, uplifting lives through support for those in need and advancing the work of organizations grounded in hope, healing, and human dignity. LB CHARITABLE FOUNDATION PURPOSE This corporation is a nonprofit public benefit corporation and is not organized for the private gain of any person. It is organized under the California Nonprofit Public Benefit Corporation Law for public and charitable purposes. The specific purposes of this corporation are to support organizations that benefit the homeless, animal shelters, cancer research, disabled veterans, drug rehabilitation clinics, and victims of gun violence, and to engage in any other lawful activities permitted under the California Nonprofit Public Benefit Corporation Law. This corporation has been formed for such public and charitable purposes only. WHO WE ARE LBCF, at its core, is a family-led foundation whose primary objectives are philanthropy and social well-being (e.g. educational, religious, or other activities serving the public interest or common good). We work together to lead the foundation and create access to opportunity for people and surrounding communities. WHAT WE DO As we set our eyes on making an impact that improves lives today in surrounding communities and organizations, we also want to ensure that it lasts to benefit future generations. That’s why we here at LBCF are committed to the issues we work on for the long term, and why we’re constantly evaluating our efforts to make sure our investments and solutions are effective and long lasting. HOW WE WORK LBCF identifies important “need” gaps in our areas of interest or communities. ## Our Mission
To provide financial support to non-profit organizations, human and health services, homeless shelters, military veterans, religious causes, humane societies, sex and human trafficking, and victims of crimes and calamities.
Our vision To serve with compassion and faith, uplifting lives through support for those in need and advancing the work of organizations grounded in hope, healing, and human dignity.
## Our vision
To serve with compassion and faith, uplifting lives through support for those in need and advancing the work of organizations grounded in hope, healing, and human dignity.
LB CHARITABLE FOUNDATION PURPOSE This corporation is a nonprofit public benefit corporation and is not organized for the private gain of any person. It is organized under the California Nonprofit Public Benefit Corporation Law for public and charitable purposes. The specific purposes of this corporation are to support organizations that benefit the homeless, animal shelters, cancer research, disabled veterans, drug rehabilitation clinics, and victims of gun violence, and to engage in any other lawful activities permitted under the California Nonprofit Public Benefit Corporation Law. This corporation has been formed for such public and charitable purposes only. WHO WE ARE LBCF, at its core, is a family-led foundation whose primary objectives are philanthropy and social well-being (e.g. educational, religious, or other activities serving the public interest or common good). We work together to lead the foundation and create access to opportunity for people and surrounding communities. WHAT WE DO As we set our eyes on making an impact that improves lives today in surrounding communities and organizations, we also want to ensure that it lasts to benefit future generations. That’s why we here at LBCF are committed to the issues we work on for the long term, and why we’re constantly evaluating our efforts to make sure our investments and solutions are effective and long lasting. HOW WE WORK LBCF identifies important “need” gaps in our areas of interest or communities. Develops and tests promising solutions through financial contribution to narrow those gaps and then broadly share new ideas with the community or leadership.
